# Comparison-based sort

## Core definition
 in java: use compareTo or compare method
``` java
	import java.util.Comparator
	public class LengthComparator implements Comparator<String> {
		public int compare(String x, String b) {
			return x.length() - b.length();
		}
	}
```
- In java, Arrays.sort() uses MergeSort(TimSort) or QuickSort
- Use "<, >, == " to compare
- lower bound: any comparison sort worse case Ω(N log N)
## Heap sort(堆)
	Naive Algorithm:
	Input: an array A[0,..., N-1]
	1. Create a new empty max-heap array H
	2. for all x in A:
		H.insert(x)
	3. Create a new array T that has size N
	4. for i from N-1 to 0:
		max = H.deleteMax()
		B[i] = max
	5. return B
[HeapSort](heapsort.md)
- Θ(NlogN)

## MergeSort
[MergeSort](mergesort.md)

### Quicksort vs. MergeSort
- Mergesort is faster than Quicksort L3S (leftmost pivot, 3-scan partition) and QuickSort PickTH (median pivot, Tony Hoare partition)
- Quicksort LTHS (leftmost pivot, Tony Hoare partition) is faster than Mergesort!

## InsertionSort

## QuickSort
[QuickSort](quicksort.md)

## Selection sort
	Algorithm: 
	1. Find the smallest item.
	2. Swap that item to the front.
	3. Repeat until all items are fixed (there are no inversions).
- for all cases: $Θ(N^2)$
